_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Conduct required reviews as appropriate within environment (e.g., Technical Surveillance, Countermeasure Reviews [TSCM], TEMPEST countermeasure reviews).
reference_document_element_description: Conduct code reviews for data application changes, evaluating code quality, test coverage, performance implications, documentation completeness, and adherence to team coding standards.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-003 (RC, cosine: 0.475), RC-K-014 (RC, cosine: 0.451).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.3/10 (raw 0.532). Shared vocabulary: [conduct, reviews]. DCWF-only terms [appropriate, countermeasure, environment, required, surveillance, technical] vs WIDAI-only terms [adherence, application, changes, code, coding, completeness] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.835 (P=0.826, R=0.843). Balanced P/R — the 16-word DCWF element and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Conduct required reviews as appropriate within environment (e.g., Technical Surv" and "Conduct code reviews for data application changes, evaluating code quality, test" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.478 < STS 0.532 (gap -0.054). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [appropriate, countermeasure, environment] and [adherence, application, changes] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.1%. Shared tokens: [conduct, reviews]. DCWF-only: [appropriate, countermeasure, environment, required, surveillance, technical]. WIDAI-only: [adherence, application, changes, code, coding, completeness].
